title: ervum exiguum
intro: Ervum exiguum, also known as the Small Trefoil or Less Clover, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Ervum exiguum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of various gastrointestinal problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and interactions.
